narrative: The temperature climbs to forty by early afternoon and the frost is gone but the clouds haven’t broken. At Tuck’s Books, Travis Kimura showed up without being asked — he heard from someone at the outfitter shop and drove over with a box of garbage bags and a pair of rubber boots. He and Amber have been carrying soggy boxes out to the alley for an hour, not talking much, which suits them both. Across town, Ryan Ostrowski gets a text in the teachers’ lounge that sends him out into the hallway to pump his fist once, privately: the Broncs made the state bracket. MetraPark tomorrow night. Petra is at her desk working through a site plan revision that arrived this morning with the client’s handwriting in the margins, which she finds both charming and maddening. Eli is at his apartment, laptop open, cursor blinking on a paragraph he wrote at six AM that he thought was good and now isn’t sure about.
events: Travis helps Amber move waterlogged books out of Tuck’s Books after hearing about the pipe burst — they work for two hours, mostly in silence
Ryan gets word that the high school basketball team made the state playoff bracket — MetraPark will host Saturday’s game
